title: transfer — compliance evidence
description: Transfer moves data across tenant boundaries at user request — imports carry data in, exports carry data out. Compliance surface is dominated by data-portability + retention + right-to-erasure. Every job transition also writes an audit row via HasAudit — the primary regulator-facing evidence is on `audits`, not here. This file documents what transfer contributes.

controls.evidence: Retention tier on xfer_jobs (90 / 180 / 365d per plan) + independent artifact TTL (7 / 14 / 30d). PruneXferArtifactsJob enforces daily. Documented in retention.json.

user::UserErased listener (AnonymiseTransferForErasedUser) rewrites causer / owner references. xfer_jobs rows retained as operational evidence with anonymised initiator. Shared mapping profiles transfer ownership.
Every domain module's #[Exportable] entity is exportable by the subject via GET /api/v1/transfer/exports — machine-readable CSV / XLSX / JSON. Signed URL delivery covers off-session download.

tenant_id in rows is always resolved from server-side context, never trusted from an uploaded file (see TRANSFER_TENANT_MISMATCH). Per-attribute #[ImportField(lookup:)] scopes FK resolution to the caller's tenant.
xfer_jobs is a light records-of-processing surface for tenants (who exported what, when). The heavy version is in `audit`.

Artifacts stored on config-selectable disks (typically S3 SSE-KMS). Signed URLs default 24h TTL. Checksums (SHA-256) recorded for tamper detection. Restricted-tier fields never emitted on the wire (see data-classes.json + x-wire.hidden).
Same export machinery serves CCPA data-subject-access-requests. `transfer:export --format=json --filter[user_id]=...` produces the machine-readable copy.
Same erasure listener as GDPR Art. 17.
Per-entity `<entity>.import` / `<entity>.export` permission composed with transfer.imports.run / transfer.exports.run. Policy enforced at both HTTP + policy layer.
Metrics in metrics.json. Health probes for storage / workers / stalled jobs. Stalled-job auto-cancel prevents silent stuck workflows.
Every xfer_job transition writes an activity row (activity module). Tenant admins see 'Alice imported 1,245 athletes' + errors count in the feed.
Import mode replace / delete require explicit entitlement + feature toggle. Not on by default.

controls.principle: privacy protection in the processing of PII
controls.evidence: tenant_id never trusted from file; per-tenant scope on lookup; restricted-tier fields never in wire payloads; artifacts on config-selectable encrypted disks.
Retention scan (PruneXferArtifactsJob) hard-deletes artifact files past TTL, hard-deletes xfer_jobs rows past retention tier, anonymises on user erasure.
